🍂 Our Mission

Seasons of Giving Lake Norman empowers youth, adults, and seniors by creating intergenerational pathways to connection, growth, and opportunity. Through compassionate service, life-skills education, and hands-on learning, we strengthen individuals, families, and the entire Lake Norman community.

🦰

Preparing Our Youth

Future Ready internships, career exploration, and soft skills from middle school through career placement.

MS → HS → Internship → Career
💼

Empowering Adults

Soft skills training, career counseling, and job placement for professionals in transition.

Soft Skills · Coaching · Placement
❤️

Honoring Our Seniors

Weekly meal delivery, Thursday activities, Tuesday resources, and Novant Health partnerships.

Wed · Thu · Tue · Novant
⭐ Our Values
✅
Consistency
Showing up builds trust
💡
Empowerment
Tools to thrive at every stage
🌈
Compassionate Excellence
Dignity in every interaction

🎓 Programs & Pathways

A Living Classroom where every generation grows together — from middle school through senior care.

🦰 Youth Future Ready Pathway
In partnership with LINK Lake Norman’s Future Ready program
🧙

Middle School Career Readiness

Career exploration, skill discovery, interests, life skills, and clarity on opportunities before high school.

🏫

High School Career Exploration

Building life and soft skills for the workforce. Preparing for real-world jobs and internships in junior year.

💼

Future Ready Internships

Real-world placements in roles attainable with 2-year, certifications, or 4-year degrees. Exposure + opportunity.

🏉

Pathway Mentorship

Building mentorship and pathways to “What’s Next” after high school — college, trade, or career.

🌿 Emotional Intelligence
💓 Active Listening
🤝 Leadership
👔 Professional Etiquette
🤖 AI Utilization
💵 Financial Literacy
📝 Resume Writing
🎤 Communication
💼 Adult Workforce Programs
🎯

Soft Skills Training

Financial literacy, conflict resolution, interview prep, AI use, resume support, and more.

🔎

Career Counseling

Coaching, mentorship, and aligned resources through LINK Lake Norman — navigate your path with purpose.

📈

Job Placement

For veterans, graduates, career changers, and workforce re-entry. Partnerships with recruiters and employers.

🏛

Community Building

Outreach focused on education, health, and social welfare — creating connection and opportunity at every level.

♥️ Serving Seniors
🚗

Weekly Meal Delivery

Every Wednesday 4:45–5:30 PM. Pickup at Huntersville UMC, deliver to 300 Huntersville Concord Rd.

🎨

Thursday Activities

Bingo, arts & crafts, book club, music, chair yoga, painting, technology help, and more every Thursday.

🏛

Tuesday Resources

Community organizations, benefits advocates, and senior services share insights every Tuesday at 1 PM.

🏥

Healthcare Partnership

No-cost well checks through Novant Health brought directly to senior communities.

🤝 Mentors, Students & Interns

The Living Classroom — where students gain real-world skills and seniors provide the mentorship that shapes our youth.

👑 Become a Mentor
🦰

Youth Mentor

Guide middle or high school students on career readiness, goal setting, and professional skills. 1–2 hrs/month.

💼

Workforce Mentor

Support adults in career transition with resume review, interview coaching, and industry networking.

📚

Senior Mentor

Our seniors ARE the mentors — pairing with youth to share wisdom, life experience, and community history.

🎓 Students & Interns
🧙

Middle School Explorers

Career exploration, skill discovery, and clarity on opportunities before high school.

Grades 6–8
🏫

High School Career Ready

Life skills, soft skills, and career readiness building up to real-world internships in junior year.

Grades 9–12
💼

Future Ready Interns

Real-world internship placements with mentorship, exposure, and career connections.

Junior year +
❤

Senior Service Volunteers

Earn service hours and build soft skills by serving seniors weekly.

All grades · Service hours
🎯 Soft Skills Training
🌿
Emotional Intelligence
Empathy, compassion, and patience through genuine senior relationships.
💓
Art of Connection
Active listening through the history and stories of our community’s seniors.
🤝
Leadership & Team Building
Real leadership in a real environment — every week.
👔
Professional Etiquette
Accountability, communication, and respect practiced every week.
🤖
AI & Tech Skills
Resume writing, AI utilization, and professional communication tools.
💵
Financial Literacy
Budgeting, saving, and planning — the fundamentals of a successful life.
📝 Apply — Mentor, Student, or Intern
Complete this form and Jaime Lane will follow up within 48 hours.

♥ Volunteer Opportunities

Three ways to serve every week. Give 2 Hours. Change a Life.

🚗 Wednesday Meal Delivery
Every Wednesday · 4:45–5:30 PM
Pickup: Huntersville United Methodist Church
Deliver to: 300 Huntersville Concord Rd, Huntersville NC
Contact: seasonsofgivinglkn@gmail.com
By registering you agree to pick up at Huntersville UMC at 4:45 PM, deliver to all recipients by 5:30 PM, and notify Jaime if you need to cancel. Instructions emailed upon acceptance.
🎨 Thursday Activities
Every Thursday · Time varies
Location: Seasons of Giving LKN, Huntersville NC
Contact: seasonsofgivinglkn@gmail.com
🏛 Tuesday Community Resources — 1:00 PM
For community orgs sharing resources with our seniors.
Contact: seasonsofgivinglkn@gmail.com

📝 General Registration

Sign up for any program or request more information. We follow up within 48 hours.
